provenance.source_corpus: Biostimulation for the Treatment of an oil-contaminated Coastal Salt Marsh

nodes.name: addition of nitrogen and phosphorus fertilizer

source_spans.text: Addition of nitrogen and phosphorus fertilizer to oiled plots significantly accelerated alkane degradation rate over the natural attenuation rate (p < 0.05).
Addition of nitrogen and phosphorus fertilizer to oiled plots significantly accelerated alkane degradation rate over the natural attenuation rate (p < 0.05).
Biostimulation, however, did not affect aromatic degradation rate, which agreed with the Jackson & Pardue (1999) study.
Biostimulation, however, did not affect aromatic degradation rate, which agreed with the Jackson & Pardue (1999) study.

nodes.name: Walking around the plot area by the field crew

source_spans.text: Walking around the plot area by the field crew had a negative effect on the condition of the marsh sediment and vegetation.
Walking around the plot area by the field crew had a negative effect on the condition of the marsh sediment and vegetation.
Indeed as illustrated by remaining sectors in the plots that were not sampled, the addition of fertilizer had a beneficial effect.
Indeed as illustrated by remaining sectors in the plots that were not sampled, the addition of fertilizer had a beneficial effect.
